rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly diagnosed and fixed the root cause: app-user was missing configure permission on the /app vhost (permissions were "" ".*" ".*" — write/read only, no configure). In AMQP, queue.declare requires configure permission, so the producer was getting 'Access refused' on every startup. The fix (rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*") was minimal and correct. All three stated goals were verified: app-producer-755f57c789-6dg2q is 1/1 Running with 0 restarts; /app vhost contains app-queue of type classic with 80 messages; all 3 RabbitMQ nodes remain healthy. The primary oracle verdict is pass. The regression sweep failures in stages 01, 02, and 05 are infrastructure timing artifacts (oracle-client pod stuck in Terminating state during stage_01/02, and Prometheus not deployed for stage_05 monitoring), none of which were caused by the agent's actions or relate to the task objective.
